paper_title: Gravity-independent Rock-climbing Robot and a Sample Acquisition Tool with Microspine Grippers
source_file: 机理文献/核心文献/20_Gravity-independent Rock-climbing Robot and a Sample Acquisition Tool with Microspine Grippers.pdf

render_review.all_pages: All 19 PDF pages were rendered to PNG at 150 dpi and visually checked in five contact sheets; retained text, equations, captions and table values were then checked on individual page renders.
render_review.notes: The three retained figure crops were cut from 260 dpi renders and checked for complete labels, force directions, dimensions and captions. Equations and Table I were verified against the page images rather than text extraction alone.

sections_checked: Section 4 Climbing Robot Kinematics and Control
Section 8 Sample Acquisition Tool Design and Experiments
Acknowledgments, multimedia note and references checked for document completeness only

equations_checked.notes: Retained as E1. The summation sign, M-plus subscript, spring moment Mk, cable tension T, reaction components Rx/Ry, signs, and moment arms d1-d3 were visually checked against Eq. (1) and Fig. 7. The paper does not append an explicit '= 0', so none was added.
Retained together as E2. R_drill = R_y1 + R_y2 and R_x1 = R_x2 were visually checked; directions and the symmetric two-carriage boundary were cross-checked against Fig. 19 on PDF p.15.

Motor-torque sizing for the ball-screw engagement actuator was inspected. It is an implementation sizing equation for the drill mechanism and does not constrain the requested spine/contact or array mechanism.

tables_checked.notes: All eight materials and the normal, 45-degree and tangent mean pull-off forces were visually checked. The four consolidated-rock rows and the loose-material range are retained. Each cell is the mean of five trials; no standard deviation or raw curve is reported.

figures_checked.reason: Robot and asteroid application images are context only and do not define the retained mechanism.

Single-spine construction and multiscale conformation were inspected; their essential hierarchy and qualitative load-sharing role are retained textually and Fig. 5 provides the more complete mechanism interface.
Irreplaceable definition of the radial carriages, serial compliance, torsion springs, prismatic slides, engage/disengage mechanisms and yaw bearing.
Assembled hardware photograph does not add a model variable beyond Fig. 5.
Required to define Eq. (1) force directions, moment arms and cable angle without guessing the coordinate system.
The three lower-bound force readings and their directions are more compactly retained as explicit parameter values; the photographs do not provide uncertainty or curves.
Mobility images and sag sequence were checked. The test angles, gravity offload and sag limitation are fully represented textually.
The center-pivot pull rig and material photographs were inspected; Table I and the stated material-failure modes preserve the minimal quantitative evidence.
Sensor architecture and representative force traces were inspected. The reported less-than-5-percent error and non-correlation result are preserved; the plot has no reusable numerical dataset.
Drilling photographs document configurations but are replaceable by the force/moment boundary notes.
Required to define the two-sided reaction forces, internal cable tension and the strict 2D symmetric boundary of Eq. (2)-(3).
Actuator CAD, hand tool and core photographs were checked; they do not add a transferable contact or load-sharing relation.

omitted_sections: Mission motivation and planetary-science narrative not affecting the gripper mechanism
PC104 electronics, VxWorks software architecture and ordinary teleoperation details
Flight-material manufacturing narrative except for the 80-point/one-level performance comparison
Sensor cost comparison and unprototyped sensor alternatives
Detailed ball-screw, gearbox, battery, drill-bit and astronaut-glove implementation
Acknowledgments, multimedia link and full references

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.10-11, Section 6 and Table I
uncertain_items.detail: The paper states that holding capacity trended with sample roughness, but it reports no 2D/3D roughness metric, friction coefficient or material-strength measurement. Roughness, friability and rock identity cannot be separated quantitatively.
The values are displayed as greater-than readings and the text gives no trial count or dispersion. They are retained only as direction-specific lower bounds, not calibration means.

One moment equation contains two rock-reaction components and represents a carriage/hook resultant. It cannot identify the internal distribution among 16 microspines without additional equilibrium and contact equations.
The paper reports that individual carriage load is not strongly correlated with external gripper load, but gives no correlation coefficient, per-carriage dataset or load-sharing probability model.

uncertain_items.location: PDF p.9 and p.17, Sections 5 and 9
uncertain_items.detail: Fully inverted mobility used 30-60 percent gravity offload depending on robot position; it is not a full-weight inverted free-climbing validation.
A fishing line reacted the moment caused by the offset drill center of mass. The gripper reacted drilling forces, but single-gripper moment capacity was not established.

known_warnings: Qualitative rock roughness and friability must not be converted into a numerical topography-to-capacity law.
Table I values are five-trial means without reported dispersion and are not red-brick parameters.
The radial gripper is not equivalent to a two-sided paired claw with a fully specified internal preload and 6D equilibrium.
Eq. (1) is a 2D carriage moment relation, not a closed single-spine or multi-spine load-sharing model.
The three-carriage sensor prototype does not establish the distribution of loads across all 16 carriages or 256 spines.
Compliance improves conformation, load sharing and vibration damping but also causes sag and hole-start wander; retaining only its beneficial role would contradict the paper.
Robot tests are open-loop and kinematically constrained; inverted mobility used partial gravity offload and full 6D climbing remained future work.
character_count_method: Count of CJK Unified Ideographs in evidence_card.md; within the allowed 3500-character extension because the paper contains separate hierarchical-mechanism, carriage-statics and whole-gripper validation evidence.
